data_coverage_summary: Tebra/Kareo SOAP and FHIR APIs has public documentation that indicates practice and patient-engagement workflows across patients, appointments, providers, referrals. Public guide plus PDF docs.
buyer_guidance: Use this surface for ambulatory and specialty-practice workflow integration, especially around scheduling and patient operations. Tebra should be treated as a fhir api surface for practice and patient-engagement workflows.
overview: Public documentation reviewed for Tebra/Kareo SOAP and FHIR APIs indicates Public guide plus PDF docs. The documented surface aligns with practice and patient-engagement workflows and covers patients, appointments, providers, referrals. Asset data is Unsupported. Telemetry data is Unsupported. Writeback is Supported.

available_data.description: The public documentation indicates practice-management workflows spanning patients, appointments, providers, referrals, and related operational records.
data_points: Operational notes, forms, or engagement records

ingest_considerations: The public source for Tebra/Kareo SOAP and FHIR APIs is documentation-oriented, so live access still depends on vendor-issued credentials, tenant configuration, or customer enablement.
This row is modeled as practice and patient-engagement workflows, so downstream integrations should scope data collection and writeback around those documented business workflows.
No exact public machine-readable JSON or YAML artifact was confirmed during this pass, so the documentation URL remains the primary evidence anchor.
